commands.review_summary:
The branch requires a matching canonical `ID: <approvalId>` header before Signal adds approval-reaction hints or registers reaction targets, and adds regressions for copied or mismatched `/approve` text.

PR surface: Source +9, Tests +77. Total +86 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level: current main's Signal send path parses outbound text for `/approve` and registers reaction targets without requiring an `ID:` header. I did not run a live Signal reproduction.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The protected `maintainer` label and mock-only proof make this a human review/proof decision rather than an automated repair candidate.

target.title: fix(signal): require approval id header for reactions
target.body:
## Summary

Harden Signal approval reaction binding so native reaction targets are only attached to canonical OpenClaw approval prompts.

## Changes

- Require a canonical `ID: <approvalId>` header before Signal extracts approval reaction bindings from outbound text.
- Require `/approve <approvalId> ...` command lines to match that header before reaction hints or targets are registered.
- Add Signal regression coverage for copied approval-command snippets and mismatched prompt IDs.

## Validation

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-signal.config.ts extensions/signal/src/approval-reactions.test.ts --reporter=dot`
- `git diff --check`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`

## Notes

Behavior addressed: Signal approval reactions now bind only to canonical approval prompts with matching IDs.
Real environment tested: local checkout on branch `757`.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-signal.config.ts extensions/signal/src/approval-reactions.test.ts --reporter=dot`.
Evidence after fix: focused Signal approval-reaction suite passed with 18 tests.
Observed result after fix: ordinary outbound snippets without canonical approval IDs do not append reaction hints or register reaction targets; canonical prompts remain covered by the existing target-mode reaction test.
What was not tested: live Signal service delivery.
